rationale: Both candidates independently surface the latent gap (the unswept hunyuan_image3_transformer.py caller of get_cache_scale), so gap_hit is true for both. But X's precision is hurt by two/three near-duplicate 'dead code' claims asserting get_cache_scale_mapper() never returns None — contradicting vLLM's actual default (returns None; only Fp8/CompressedTensors-style configs override it), so the guard is not dead code, and X states this with fabricated confidence and citations. Y's findings (outer-AutoWeightsLoader mechanics, the delegated-loader test bypassing the mapper it claims to test, qwen2_old's getattr guard being cleanup not regression) are all accurate, closely track the human reviewer's actual concerns (recall), and are verified against a real upstream checkout rather than asserted. Both are reasonably actionable with file/line pointers, giving Y a clear win on precision and recall despite comparable actionability and identical gap_hit.
